title: scaffold-spec output data
description:
Shape of the ``data`` field on a ``scaffold-spec`` envelope.

The agent reads ``spec``, overrides the ``unresolved_fields`` (the
handful of values context could not supply — surfaced as schema-valid
placeholders), then invokes the target verb with the result. One
scaffold + one edit + one invoke, instead of N rounds of
``spec_invalid`` feedback.

verb.description: The target verb this skeleton is for (e.g. `resolve-submit-inputs`).
verb.title: Verb

spec.description: The populated, schema-valid skeleton. Already validated against the target verb's input model, so the verb will not reject its shape — pass it (after filling `unresolved_fields`) to `hpc-agent <verb> --spec`.
spec.title: Spec

unresolved_fields.description: Dotted paths (e.g. `submit.ssh_target`, `sidecar.executor`) whose values are PLACEHOLDERS context could not supply. The caller must fill or override these before invoking the target verb — they are schema-valid so the skeleton validates, but they are not real.
unresolved_fields.title: Unresolved Fields

sources.description: Per-field provenance: which context source populated each value (`clusters.yaml#<cluster>.ssh_target`, `compute-run-id(<run>)`, `load-context latest_run.*`), or a `placeholder — ...` marker for the unresolved ones.
sources.title: Sources

warnings.description: Context-gathering degradations (e.g. clusters.yaml unreadable, or `.hpc/tasks.py` absent so run_id/cmd_sha came back as placeholders).
warnings.title: Warnings
